sections.summary: States the Code's objective: to establish principles for ethical conduct of international food trade to protect consumer health and ensure fair trade practices.

Defines the Code's scope as applying to all food introduced into international trade, including concessional and food aid transactions, and calls for governments to promote ethical conduct nationally with other parties.

States principles that international food trade must ensure consumers safe, sound and wholesome food and prohibits trade in foods that are hazardous to health, filthy/decomposed, adulterated, falsely labelled or presented, prepared/handled under unsanitary conditions, or have unsuitable expiration dating.

sections.section: ARTICLE 4 CONDITIONS NECESSARY FOR FOOD IN INTERNATIONAL TRADE
sections.summary: Requires competent authorities to apply the Code's ethical principles; generally prohibits export/re-export of food not complying with the exporting country's legislation unless importing-country legislation or competent authority acceptance provides otherwise (taking Codex standards into account), forbids placing unsafe or unsuitable food into international trade for disposal, and reminds national authorities of IHR (2005) notification/reporting duties and obligations to observe the Code on marketing of breast-milk substitutes and WHA resolutions.
